en_title: 'It Never Trickles Down' — Bernie Sanders Hits Out at Microsoft Over Xbox Layoffs and Console Price Rises, Says They Prove Corporate Tax Breaks Do Not Create Jobs
cn_title: 伯尼·桑德斯怒批微软XBOX裁员涨价
subtitle: 滴漏神话
opus_summary: 美国参议员伯尼·桑德斯批评微软在获得巨额减税、赚取高额利润后仍裁撤XBOX员工并上调主机价格，称这证明企业减税不会创造就业。

paragraphs.en: Sen. Bernie Sanders has hit out at Microsoft over the recent layoffs at Xbox as well as console price rises, insisting they’re evidence that corporate tax breaks do not create jobs.
paragraphs.cn: 美国参议员伯尼·桑德斯批评微软近期在XBOX裁员以及上调主机价格，并坚称这些事情证明企业减税不会创造就业。

paragraphs.en: Pointing to the billions of dollars of profit Microsoft makes alongside the billions in tax breaks it has received and the tens of millions it pays CEO Satya Nadella, the 84-year-old senator from Vermont claimed that the layoffs were proof that corporate tax breaks do not lead to job creation.
paragraphs.cn: 这位84岁的佛蒙特州参议员指出，微软赚取了数千亿美元利润，同时获得数百亿美元减税，还向CEO Satya Nadella支付数千万美元薪酬。他认为，这次裁员证明企业减税不会带来就业创造。

paragraphs.en: “Last year, Microsoft made $101 billion in profits, got a $12.5 billion tax break from Trump & paid its CEO $96 million,” Sanders tweeted. “This year, it’s raising the price of an Xbox by $150 & eliminating 3,200 jobs. Please don’t tell me corporate tax breaks create jobs. It never trickles down.”
paragraphs.cn: 桑德斯在推文中写道：「去年，微软赚取1010亿美元(约合人民币6870亿元)利润，从特朗普那里获得125亿美元(约合人民币850亿元)减税，并向CEO支付9600万美元(约合人民币6.5亿元)。今年，它把一台XBOX涨价150美元(约合人民币1020元)，并裁掉3200个岗位。请不要告诉我企业减税会创造就业。它永远不会向下滴漏。」

paragraphs.en: This week, Microsoft announced 3,200 layoffs, with 1,600 going now and the rest to follow — leaving thousands of employees with an anxious wait that could drag out for months. As part of the cuts, Microsoft has jettisoned four studios which were brought in to bolster Game Pass with new games under the Phil Spencer era, with another studio in negotiations to be sold or shut down.
paragraphs.cn: 本周，微软宣布裁员3200人，其中1600人立即离职，其余裁员将在之后发生。这让数千名员工陷入可能持续数月的焦虑等待。作为裁员的一部分，微软已经剥离了4家工作室；这些工作室是在菲尔·斯宾塞时代被纳入，用来以新游戏强化Game Pass。另有一家工作室正在谈判出售或关闭。

paragraphs.en: The news followed the announcement of a significant price hike for Xbox consoles, which Microsoft blamed on the ongoing “components crisis.” The price rises, which start from August 1, will see the Xbox Series increase by $100 for 512GB models and $150 for 1TB models. The 2TB model will be discontinued.
paragraphs.cn: 在裁员消息之前，微软刚宣布XBOX主机将大幅涨价，并将原因归咎于持续存在的「组件危机」。涨价将从8月1日开始，XBOX Series 512GB机型上调100美元(约合人民币680元)，1TB机型上调150美元(约合人民币1020元)。2TB机型将停产。

paragraphs.en: In its 2025 financial year (ending June 30, 2025), Microsoft reported a record net income of $101.8 billion (a 16% increase year-over-year). This massive profit was generated on $281.7 billion in total revenue, driven by broad growth across Microsoft’s cloud, AI, and productivity segments.
paragraphs.cn: 在截至2025年6月30日的2025财年，微软报告净利润达到创纪录的1018亿美元(约合人民币6922亿元)，同比增长16%。这笔巨额利润来自2817亿美元(约合人民币1.9万亿元)总营收，推动因素是微软云、AI和生产力业务的广泛增长。

paragraphs.en: Sharma has said the Xbox business, however, is struggling. Xbox ended the financial year with a 3% accountability margin (assumed to be profit margin), down year-over-year. Excluding Activision Blizzard King, over the past five years, Microsoft spent over $20 billion on ongoing investments in what Sharma called its content, platform, and hardware “subsidy,” but annual revenue has declined nearly half a billion during that time.
paragraphs.cn: 不过，Sharma表示，XBOX业务正在挣扎。XBOX以3%的责任利润率（推测为利润率）结束这一财年，同比下降。不计动视暴雪King，过去5年里，微软在Sharma所称的内容、平台和硬件「补贴」上持续投入超过200亿美元(约合人民币1360亿元)，但同期年收入下降了近5亿美元(约合人民币34亿元)。

paragraphs.en: “We are operating at margins that are 3-10x lower than comparable platform and publishing businesses,” Sharma said in a blog post announcing the layoffs. “We entered Gen 9 with a smaller install base and a higher cost structure. To grow, we bet on Game Pass, multi-platform, and a broader portfolio of content. While those businesses have created meaningful value, they did not grow at the pace we expected. As that happened, our core business weakened, and we added more teams, more investment, and more time, hoping for a better outcome. And now the industry is facing the most severe hardware crisis in its history. We must reset Xbox.”
paragraphs.cn: Sharma在宣布裁员的博客文章中说：「我们的利润率比可比的平台和发行企业低3到10倍。进入第9世代时，我们的装机基础更小，成本结构更高。为了增长，我们押注Game Pass、多平台和更广泛的内容组合。虽然这些业务创造了有意义的价值，但它们没有按我们预期的速度增长。随着这种情况发生，我们的核心业务变弱，而我们增加了更多团队、更多投资和更多时间，希望取得更好的结果。现在，行业正面临史上最严重的硬件危机。我们必须重置XBOX。」

paragraphs.en: And on Xbox console price rises, Microsoft has said console storage and memory prices have increased by more than 2.5x, and it expects another doubling by the fall of 2027. “The entire consumer electronics industry is struggling with the current components crisis, but the effects are particularly hard on consoles,” Microsoft said when it announced the price rises.
paragraphs.cn: 关于XBOX主机涨价，微软表示主机存储和内存价格已经上涨超过2.5倍，并预计到2027年秋季还会再翻一番。微软在宣布涨价时表示：「整个消费电子行业都在当前组件危机中挣扎，但这种影响对主机尤其严重。」

paragraphs.en: “… the industry is facing the most severe hardware crisis in its history,” Sharma added in the Xbox “Reset” memo.
paragraphs.cn: Sharma也在XBOX「重置」备忘录中补充说：「……行业正面临史上最严重的硬件危机。」

paragraphs.en: Of course, Sanders’ statement is less about Xbox specifically, and more about Microsoft and his views on corporate America. But it’s worth noting that this week’s layoffs at Xbox were a part of broader cuts across Microsoft. The company actually announced plans to cut 4,800 jobs — roughly 2.1% of its workforce — although clearly Xbox is bearing the brunt of the restructure.
paragraphs.cn: 当然，桑德斯的表态并不完全针对XBOX本身，更多是针对微软以及他对美国企业界的看法。但值得注意的是，本周XBOX裁员只是微软更广泛裁员的一部分。微软实际上宣布计划裁撤4800个岗位，约占员工总数2.1%，不过XBOX显然承担了这次重组的主要冲击。

paragraphs.en: This isn’t the first time Sanders has spoken out about the goings on in the video game industry. In 2022, Sanders and three other U.S. senators raised concerns about Microsoft’s then proposed acquisition of Activision Blizzard. “We are deeply concerned about consolidation in the tech industry and its impact on workers,” Elizabeth Warren, Bernie Sanders, Cory Booker, and Sheldon Whitehouse wrote.
paragraphs.cn: 这并不是桑德斯第一次就游戏行业事件发声。2022年，桑德斯和另外3名美国参议员曾对微软当时拟议收购动视暴雪一事表达担忧。Elizabeth Warren、伯尼·桑德斯、Cory Booker和Sheldon Whitehouse当时写道：「我们对科技行业整合及其对劳动者的影响深感担忧。」

paragraphs.en: Microsoft eventually won out in its battle with the FTC over the $69 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard. That enormous buyout was meant to help Xbox grow its gaming business significantly. This week, Sharma admitted it had been a failure.
paragraphs.cn: 微软最终在围绕690亿美元(约合人民币4692亿元)收购动视暴雪的FTC诉讼中胜出。这笔巨额收购原本是为了帮助XBOX大幅发展游戏业务。本周，Sharma承认这一策略已经失败。

paragraphs.en: Responding to the layoffs, the Communications Workers of America (CWA) said they would fight for the rights of affected Xbox staff.
paragraphs.cn: 针对裁员，美国通信工人协会（CWA）表示，他们将为受影响XBOX员工的权利而斗争。

paragraphs.en: “When Microsoft sought to grow its video game division, corporate executives made an agreement with video game workers and their union, CWA, to respect their right to organize for a new day for workers across Microsoft’s video game studios,” Claude Cummings Jr., President of CWA, said. “Although our union signed neutrality agreements with Microsoft, we have been extremely disappointed by a company that has slow-walked our members at the bargaining table, making CWA members wait for the protections of a union contract.
paragraphs.cn: CWA主席Claude Cummings Jr.说：「当微软试图扩大其电子游戏部门时，公司高管与电子游戏从业者及其工会CWA达成协议，尊重他们在微软各电子游戏工作室组织工会、为劳动者迎来新一天的权利。虽然我们的工会与微软签署了中立协议，但这家公司在谈判桌上拖延我们的成员、让CWA成员等待工会合同保护的做法，让我们极其失望。」

paragraphs.en: “But make no mistake — whether our members have a contract in hand, or are still at the bargaining table, CWA members at Xbox have the power and protection of union membership. When Microsoft decides to treat the workers who built Xbox as expendable, it should know who they’re dealing with. This is not just a fight with the thousands of workers across Xbox; it’s a fight with each and every member at CWA — hundreds of thousands of people strong.”
paragraphs.cn: 他继续说：「但不要误解，无论我们的成员已经拿到合同，还是仍在谈判桌前，XBOX的CWA成员都拥有工会成员身份带来的力量和保护。当微软决定把打造XBOX的劳动者视为可牺牲品时，它应该知道自己面对的是谁。这不只是与XBOX数千名劳动者的斗争，而是与CWA每一位成员的斗争，背后有数十万人。」

pending_dict.reason: 词库未收录；为避免误译，正文暂保留英文名。
